'''''Noche UFC: Lopes vs. Silva''''' (also known as '''''UFC Fight Night 259''''', '''''Noche UFC 3''''' and '''''UFC on ESPN+ 117''''') was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on September 13, 2025, at the [[Frost Bank Center]] in [[San Antonio, Texas]], United States.<ref name="SA">{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2025/05/31/noche-ufc-moved-from-guadalajara-to-san-antonio-texas/|title=Noche UFC moved from Guadalajara to San Antonio, Texas|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Eddie Law|date=2025-05-31|access-date=2025-05-31}}</ref>

==Background==
The event marked the promotion's fourth visit to San Antonio and first since [[UFC on ESPN: Vera vs. Sandhagen]] in March 2023.<ref name="SA"/>

It was the third annual "Noche UFC" event commemorating [[Independence Day (Mexico)|Mexico's independence day]], following September 2023's [[UFC Fight Night: Grasso vs. Shevchenko 2]] and September 2024's [[UFC 306]]. The card was originally expected to take place in [[Guadalajara]], Mexico and serve as UFC 320, but it was moved to San Antonio (and shifted to a Fight Night event) due to the construction of Arena Guadalajara being stalled.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/03/ufc-320-noche-guadalajara-mexico-announcement|title=Noche UFC heads to Mexico for first time as UFC 320 set for Guadalajara|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-03-29|access-date=2025-03-29}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/5/31/24440859/noche-ufc-3-moves-from-guadalajara-mexico-to-san-antonio|title=Noche UFC 3 moves from Guadalajara, Mexico to San Antonio|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2025-05-31|access-date=2025-05-31}}</ref>

A featherweight bout between former [[UFC Featherweight Championship]] challenger [[Diego Lopes (fighter)|Diego Lopes]] and [[Jean Silva]] headlined the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/6/24/24455260/diego-lopes-vs-jean-silva-booked-as-main-event-for-latest-noche-ufc|title=Diego Lopes vs. Jean Silva booked as main event for latest Noche UFC|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2025-06-24|access-date=2025-06-24}}</ref>

[[Rob Font]] and [[Raul Rosas Jr.]] were scheduled to meet in a bantamweight bout in the co-main event.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Rob Font kontra Raul Rosas Jr. na wrześniowej gali Noche UFC|url=https://www.mmarocks.pl/ufc/rob-font-kontra-raul-rosas-jr-na-wrzesniowej-gali-noche-ufc|access-date=2025-07-03|publisher=mmarocks.pl|author=Bartłomiej Zubkiewicz}} {{in lang|pl}}</ref> However, Rosas Jr. withdrew due to a rib injury and was replaced by [[David Martínez (fighter)|David Martínez]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.telecomasia.net/news/mma/rosas-withdraws-from-bout-with-font-at-noche-ufc|title=Rosas withdraws from bout with Font at Noche UFC|publisher=telecomasia.net|author=Marina Magomedova|date=2025-08-28|access-date=2025-08-28}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/09/01/noche-ufc-raul-rosas-jr-blames-rib-injury-co-main-event-pull-out-rob-font-david-martinez/85930735007|title=Raul Rosas Jr. says rib injury led to Noche UFC co-main event pull-out|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2025-09-01|access-date=2025-09-01}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/08/30/noche-ufc-david-martinez-replaces-raul-rosas-jr-vs-rob-font/85904238007/|title=Rob Font, David Martinez agree to new Noche UFC co-main event|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-08-30|access-date=2025-08-30}}</ref> Martínez was originally expected to face Carlos Vera at this event, before the latter withdrew and was replaced by Quang Le.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Carlos Vera volverá al octágono en septiembre y se enfrentará a David Martínez|url=https://www.eldiario.ec/centro/carlos-vera-volvera-al-octagono-en-septiembre-y-se-enfrentara-a-david-martinez-20250725/|date=2025-07-25|access-date=2025-07-31|publisher=eldiario.ec|author=Julio Moreira}} {{in lang|es}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|author=Alexander Behunin|date=2025-08-21|title=Chiwiwis! Raul Rosas Jr. next fight booked, battles veteran Rob Font to help bolster rudderless Noche UFC 3 card|url=https://www.mmamania.com/ufc-fight-announcements/384027/chiwiwis-raul-rosas-jr-next-fight-booked-battles-veteran-rob-font-to-help-bolster-rudderless-noche-ufc-3-card|access-date=2025-08-21|publisher=mmamania.com}}</ref> Following Martínez's reassignment to the co-main event, Le was matched with promotional newcomer [[Santiago Luna (fighter)|Santiago Luna]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.si.com/fannation/mma/news/ufc-21-year-old-fighter-two-weeks-notice-noche-ufc-santiago-luna|title=UFC calls in 21-year-old fighter on less than two weeks' notice for Noche UFC debut|publisher=si.com|author=Drew Beaupre|date=2025-09-03|access-date=2025-09-03}}</ref>

A women's strawweight bout between former [[UFC Women's Strawweight Championship]] challengers [[Amanda Lemos]] and [[Tatiana Suarez]] (also [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Joanna vs. Team Cláudia]] strawweight winner) took place at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.si.com/fannation/mma/news/tatiana-suarez-amanda-lemos-pivotal-noche-ufc-fight|title=Former title challengers booked for pivotal Noche UFC fight|publisher=si.com|author=Drew Beaupre|date=2025-07-23|access-date=2025-07-23}}</ref> They were originally scheduled to meet at [[UFC 298]] in February 2024, but Suarez withdrew from the event due to an injury.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Newswire|date=2024-01-16|title=Tatiana Suarez out of UFC 298, Mackenzie Dern now faces Amanda Lemos|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/1/16/24040758/tatiana-suarez-out-of-ufc-298-mackenzie-dern-now-faces-amanda-lemos|access-date=2024-01-17|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref>

Sedriques Dumas and [[Zachary Reese]] met in a middleweight bout at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Alexander Behunin|date=2025-07-23|title=Recently jailed UFC fighter booked for another fight at Noche UFC 3|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2025/7/23/24473395/recently-jailed-ufc-fighter-sedriques-dumas-zachary-reese-booked-for-another-fight-at-noche-ufc-3|access-date=2025-07-23|publisher=mmamania.com}}</ref> They were originally scheduled to meet at [[UFC 311]] in January, but Dumas withdrew from the event for unknown reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/01/ufc-311-lfa-champion-replacement-fight-azamat-bekoev|title=LFA champion gets late UFC 311 opportunity as replacement fighter|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-01-13|access-date=2025-01-13}}</ref>

[[Édgar Cháirez]] was expected to face Alessandro Costa in a flyweight bout at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://lineadirectaportal.com/deportes/de-regreso-edgar-chairez-enfrentara-a-alessandro-costa-en-noche-ufc-2025-07-23__1439808|title=¡De regreso! Édgar Cháirez enfrentará a Alessandro Costa en Noche UFC|publisher=lineadirectaportal.com|author=Oscar Arrieta|date=2025-07-23|access-date=2025-07-30}} {{in lang|es}}</ref> However, Cháirez pulled out in late July due to a broken leg and was replaced by Alden Coria.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.record.com.mx/extremo/edgar-chairez-se-perdera-la-noche-de-ufc-por-lesion-en-el-pie#google_vignette|title=Edgar Chairez se perderá la Noche de UFC por lesión en el pie|publisher=record.com.mx|author=Mario Ángel Guzmán Domínguez|date=2025-07-29|access-date=2025-07-30}} {{in lang|es}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2025-08-04|title='Unreal' … UFC's newest fighter Alden Coria reacts to short notice Noche UFC debut in home state|url=https://bloodyelbow.com/2025/08/04/unreal-ufcs-newest-fighter-alden-coria-reacts-to-short-notice-noche-ufc-debut-in-home-state/|access-date=2025-07-31|publisher=bloodyelbow.com|author=Christopher De Santiago}}</ref>

A women's bantamweight bout between former [[UFC Women's Bantamweight Championship|UFC Women's Bantamweight Champion]] [[Raquel Pennington]] and [[Norma Dumont]] was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/ExChamp-19YearOld-Golden-Girl-Among-Those-Expected-for-Noche-UFC-197631|title=Ex-Champ, 19-Year-Old 'Golden Girl' Among Those Expected for Noche UFC|publisher=sherdog.com|author=Tristen Critchfield|date=2025-07-08|access-date=2025-08-07}}</ref> However, Pennington had to withdraw due to an undisclosed injury, so the bout was removed from the card.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/latest-news/308039/raquel-pennington-reveals-shes-out-of-fight-against-norma-dumont-at-noche-ufc-3|title=Raquel Pennington reveals she's out of fight against Norma Dumont at Noche UFC 3|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2025-08-07|access-date=2025-08-07}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/398691/norma-dumont-new-ufc-fight-booked-raquel-pennington-cancellation-ketlen-vieira|title=Norma Dumont gets new UFC fight booked after Raquel Pennington cancellation|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2025-08-13|access-date=2025-08-13}}</ref>

The welterweight final of [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Cormier vs. Team Sonnen]] between Rodrigo Sezinando and Daniil Donchenko took place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmanews.com/news/ufc/rodrigo-sezinando-injured-tuf-welterweight-finale-moved-from-ufc-319-to-noche-ufc-3/|title=Rodrigo Sezinando Injured, TUF Welterweight Finale Moved From UFC 319 To Noche UFC 3|publisher=sports.yahoo.com|author=Thomas Albano|date=2025-08-14|access-date=2025-08-15}}</ref> They were initially scheduled to compete at [[UFC 319]] one month before, but the bout was shifted to this card due to an injury suffered from Sezinando's side.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/08/14/ufc-319-tuf-33-final-donchenko-vs-sezinando-canceled/85656246007|title=Injury scraps Daniil Donchenko vs. Rodrigo Sezinando 'TUF 33' final at UFC 319|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun and Nolan King|date=2025-08-14|access-date=2025-08-14}}</ref>

At the weigh-ins, former interim [[UFC Middleweight Championship]] challenger (also [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Jones vs. Team Sonnen]] middleweight winner) [[Kelvin Gastelum]] weighed in at 191 pounds, five pounds over the middleweight non-title fight limit. The bout proceeded at catchweight and he was fined 35 percent of his purse which went to his opponent [[Dustin Stoltzfus]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2025-09-12|title=Noche UFC weigh-in results: Kelvin Gastelum misses weight by 5 pounds|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/403508/noche-ufc-weigh-in-results-kelvin-gastelum-misses-weight-by-5-pounds|access-date=2025-09-12|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref>

== Bonus awards ==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bjpenn.com/mma-news/noche-ufc/noche-ufc-3-bonus-report-diego-lopes-takes-home-two-bonuses/|title=Noche UFC 3 Bonus Report: Diego Lopes takes home two bonuses|publisher=bjpenn.com|author=Chris Taylor|date=2025-09-13|access-date=2025-09-13}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Diego Lopes vs. Jean Silva'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Diego Lopes''' and '''Santiago Luna'''
